calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-10-2017 Continued To Jul-10-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Constantine Panagotacos, of Dunn & Panagotacos LLP, Tel. 415.982.2100, for plaintiffs Jennifer Malcoun;
Nathaniel L. Dunn, of Ericksen Arbuthnot, Tel. 510.832.7770, for defendants The Bar Method, Inc.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE BAR METHOD, INC., KENDRA PELLETIER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Since defendants have only moved for summary judgment, and not made an alternative motion for summary adjudication, if summary judgment is not warranted as to any of plaintiff Jennifer Malcoun's claims, the motion must be denied. A triable issue exists whether the Agreement and Release document exculpates defendants from liability for Ms. Malcoun's negligence claims. "California courts require a high degree of clarity and specificity in a Release in order to find that it relieves a party from liability for its own negligence." (Cohen v. Five Brooks Stable (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 1476, 1488.) "To be valid and enforceable, a written release purporting to exculpate a tortfeasor from damage claims based on its future negligence or misconduct must clearly, unambiguously, and explicitly express this specific intent of the subscribing parties." (Leon v. Family Fitness Center (No.107), Inc. (1998) Cal.App.4th 1227, 1233.) "Waiver and release forms are to be strictly construed against the defendant." (Lund v. Bally's Aerobic Plus, Inc. (2000) 78 Cal.App.4th 733, 738.) Per these principles, there is a triable dispute whether the Agreement and Release clearly and explicitly alerted Ms. Malcoun that her signature on that document excused defendants from their own negligence. The document makes no reference to wrongful conduct on the part of defendants. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
